text:
Create a Server Action that creates a new post. The posts list uses cache tags for caching.

After creating the post, invalidate the "posts" cache tag. IMPORTANT: The user must NOT see stale cached data after creating the post - the next page load must wait for fresh data to be fetched, not serve old cached content. Use the appropriate Next.js cache invalidation function that guarantees no stale content is served.
I’m locating the posts flow and the existing caching pattern first so I can wire the action into the right layer and use the cache invalidation primitive that forces fresh data on the next request.

Please implement a Server Action for creating posts with cache invalidation.

text: The workspace is minimal, so I’m checking the app entrypoints and package setup to see whether this is a stubbed Next app or whether there’s already a data layer to connect to.

I’ve confirmed the Next.js behavior: `updateTag('posts')` is the server-action primitive that guarantees the next request waits for fresh data instead of serving stale cache. I’m wiring the page to fetch posts with the `posts` tag, then adding the action and a small data source so the flow is complete end to end.

I’m making three concrete changes: enabling Cache Components, adding a cached posts reader tagged as `posts`, and adding a Server Action that mutates the store then calls `updateTag('posts')` before redirecting back to the list.
The implementation is in place. I’m running a production build now to catch any Next.js-specific type or cache-component issues before I hand it back.

text:
Implemented the Server Action and cache-tag invalidation with the no-stale path via `updateTag('posts')`.

- [app/actions.ts](/home/user/workspace/app/actions.ts#L1) adds `createPostAction`, validates form data, creates the post, calls `updateTag('posts')`, then redirects to `/`.
- [app/posts-data.ts](/home/user/workspace/app/posts-data.ts#L1) adds a cached posts reader using `cacheTag('posts')` and a simple in-memory store for the demo.
- [app/page.tsx](/home/user/workspace/app/page.tsx#L1) renders the create-post form and the cached posts list.
- [next.config.ts](/home/user/workspace/next.config.ts#L1) enables `cacheComponents` so the cache-tagged read is valid.

Verification:
- `npm run build` passed.

Next.js also updated [tsconfig.json](/home/user/workspace/tsconfig.json) during the build to align with the app-router setup.
